case_title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. NICHOLE CORNEJO ET AL

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 62475597) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC HEARING SET FOR OCT-24-2018 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED IN FULL. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. SEE CCP 2023.040. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER ATTACHING A COPY OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, AS AN EXHIBIT TO THE ORDER AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM PETER CATALANOTTI AT PCATALANOTTI@FMGLAS.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Department 206 for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up.
Department 514, Judge: Gail Dekreon, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Roger E. Jackson, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff Midland Funding, LLC.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendant Nichole Cornejo.
Declaration pursuant to CCP 98, submitted in lieu of testimony.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-10 dismissed without prejudice.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff Midland Funding, LLC and against Defendant Nichole Cornejo as follows:
Principal $ 2446.64
Pre-Judgment Interest $0.00
Attorney's Fees $0.00
Costs $0.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $2446.64

Judgment signed in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Assigned To Trial Court

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Set For Trial On Mar-18-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions

Plaintiff Midland Funding LLC's unopposed Motion for Order Establishing Admissions is GRANTED in full. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. See CCP 2023.040. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of order attaching a copy of Requests for Admissions, Set One, as an exhibit to the order and complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to the Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti at pcatalanotti@fmglas.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. =(302/JPT)
